title: Safety Manager
description:
**About Four Seasons:**

Four Seasons is powered by our people. We are a collective of individuals who crave to become better, to push ourselves to new heights and to treat each other as we wish to be treated in return. Our team members around the world create amazing experiences for our guests, residents, and partners through a commitment to luxury with genuine heart. We know that the best way to enable our people to deliver these exceptional guest experiences is through a world\-class employee experience and company culture.
  

  

At Four Seasons, we believe in recognizing a familiar face, welcoming a new one and treating everyone we meet the way we would want to be treated ourselves. Whether you work with us, stay with us, live with us or discover with us, we believe our purpose is to create impressions that will stay with you for a lifetime. It comes from our belief that life is richer when we truly connect to the people and the world around us.


**About the location:**

Overlooking the Venetian Lagoon and just steps from Piazza San Marco, Danieli, a Four Seasons Hotel, Venice is an iconic property offering elegant, spacious accommodations. The historic hotel showcases majestic Venetian architecture dating back to the 14th century. Guests can also enjoy spectacular views of Venice’s landmarks from the rooftop Restaurant Terrazza Danieli.
**About the Role**
------------------

As Security Manager, you will oversee all aspects of corporate security, safety, loss prevention, and emergency preparedness, ensuring the protection of employees, assets, facilities, and information.
  

Working closely with the leadership team, you will supervise daily security operations, maintain regulatory compliance, and contribute to creating a safe, efficient, and resilient workplace environment.


**What You Will Do**

* Manage and oversee external security contractors, ensuring compliance, professionalism, and a collaborative relationship while avoiding any risk of unlawful interposition.
* Supervise and coordinate daily security activities related to access control, surveillance, internal operations, and incident handling.
* Develop, implement, and maintain emergency response, crisis management, and business continuity procedures.
* Ensure compliance with national laws, workplace safety regulations, and internal corporate standards.
* Maintain effective relationships with public authorities, emergency services, and third‑party partners.
* Conduct risk assessments, facility checks, and regular inspections to identify vulnerabilities and propose corrective actions.
* Review and analyse incident reports, identifying trends and areas for improvement.
* Support company‑wide security awareness initiatives and onboarding programs for new hires.
* Organize and deliver mandatory safety and security training in accordance with Italian regulatory requirements.
* Uphold the highest levels of confidentiality, professionalism, and discretion when managing sensitive information.
* Collaborate with leadership to foster a culture of prevention, responsibility, and operational excellence.

**What You Bring**

* Degree in Security Management, Law Enforcement, Risk Management, or a related discipline.
* Solid knowledge of Italian security regulations, fire safety codes, and emergency procedures.
* Valid qualification enabling the role of safety/security trainer, in line with applicable Italian law.
* Minimum 3–5 years of experience in corporate, industrial, or large‑facility security management, with proven supervisory responsibilities.
* Experience in structured environments where high standards of compliance, confidentiality, and reliability are essential.
* Strong leadership, risk‑assessment, and problem‑solving abilities, with the capacity to remain effective under pressure.
* High integrity, cultural sensitivity, and the ability to manage confidential information responsibly.
* Proficiency with MS Office, access control systems, surveillance technologies, and incident management tools.
* Flexibility to work irregular shifts as needed, including evenings, weekends, or during crisis situations.
* Fluency in English and Italian and legal permission to work in Italy.

**What We Offer**

* Competitive Salary, wages, and a comprehensive benefits package.
* Excellent Training and Development opportunities.
* Employee Discount for stays at any Four Seasons worldwide.
* Complimentary Dry Cleaning for Employee Uniforms.
* Complimentary Employee Meals.

**Schedule \& Location**

* Full\-time position based at Hotel Danieli, Venice.
* Flexibility to work weekends and holidays as needed.
* You must possess the legal right to work in Italy and have fluency in English and Italian.

title: Sales Advisor - Settore Business Consulting
description:
**Per primaria realtà in ambito business consulting con sede a Verona, stiamo cercando una persona che apra nuove porte commerciali** 

* **Compenso su base fissa \+ provvigioni \- Modalità di lavoro ibrida.**

Se ti piace capire i business prima di vendere soluzioni, continua a leggere.

**COSA FARAI**

* Identificare e contattare imprenditori in target (da nostra lead genration, contatti qualificati \+ LinkedIn, eventi, referral, outbound).
* Condurre il primo incontro, qualificare le esigenze e posizionare i servizi della società.
* Gestire l’intero ciclo di vendita: dal contatto alla firma.
* Co\-costruire proposte commerciali su misura con il team consulting.
* Sviluppare il portafoglio clienti con upselling e cross\-selling.

**REQUISITI NECESSARI**

* 3\+ anni nella vendita B2B di servizi (consulenza, formazione, digital, servizi professionali).
* Sai gestire cicli di vendita complessi con interlocutori C\-level.
* Vendita consultiva: domande prima, soluzioni poi.

**NICE TO HAVE**

* Conoscenza del tessuto imprenditoriale del Nord Italia.
* Esperienza in consulenza strategica, marketing o sviluppo d’impresa.
* Familiarità con CRM e outbound strutturato (Sales Navigator, email automation).

**NON FA PER TE SE:**

Vendi a catalogo senza personalizzare. Misuri il successo solo in provvigioni e non in relazioni costruite.

**COSA OFFRIAMO**

* Fisso \+ variabile legato ai risultati.
* Modalità ibrida: libertà sul territorio, allineamenti in sede a Verona.
* Formazione continua su tutte le aree di competenza del team.
* Fase di espansione: entri ora, costruisci da protagonista.
* Cultura diretta: ascolto, trasparenza, zero burocrazia.

***Il vero benefit: non vendi un servizio standardizzato. Hai un team multidisciplinare alle spalle che costruisce soluzioni reali. Il tuo lavoro ha un impatto visibile sulla crescita delle imprese che segui.***

**PROCESSO DI SELEZIONE**

Candidatura → Screening telefonico (15 min) → Colloquio con i Founder → Business case pratico → Offerta.

**Ti ci ritrovi? Non aspettare.**

**Compila il questionario e candidati oggi (fai copia e incolla sul tuo browser del seguente link).**

**https://www.serotoninahr.it/zed**

Retribuzione: €1\.500,00 \- €4\.500,00 al mese

Sede di lavoro: Ibrido/da remoto (Verona, Veneto)

title: Impiegato/a Amministrativo/a
Operatore / Operatrice del Mercato del Lavoro (OML) - Vicenza
SAINT LAURENT Addetto/a distinta base - Calzature Saint Laurent
description:
**Summary**

**Job Description**

**A PROPOSITO DI SAINT LAURENT**

Fondata nel 1961, Yves Saint Laurent è stata la prima casa di moda a lanciare il concetto del prêt\-à\-porter di lusso, creando nel 1966 la linea ‘Rive Gauche’, sinonimo di gioventù e libertà. Questo cambio ha rappresentato un primo passo cruciale nella modernizzazione della moda e ha rivoluzionato il paesaggio socio\-culturale.


Nel 1999, la divisione lusso del Gruppo Kering ha acquisito la maison Yves Saint Laurent e, sotto la direzione creativa di Anthony Vaccarello dal aprile 2016, continua a posizionarla ai vertici dell’universo del lusso.


Oggi le collezioni Saint Laurent includono prêt\-à\-porter donna e uomo, scarpe, borse, piccola pelletteria, gioielli, foulard, cravatte e occhiali.


**RUOLO**

Il/la candidato/a sarà inserito all’interno dello Ufficio Sviluppo Prodotto e avrà la responsabilità di gestire le attività connesse alla creazione e manutenzione delle distinte basi per il prodotto calzature.


**MISSIONE**

* Creazione e modifica distinte basi a sistema (PLM/Stealth 3000\) in stretta collaborazione con l’ufficio sviluppo prodotto ed Industrializzazione
* Aggiornamento distinte basi post\-industrializzazione
* Controllo ed analisi dei dati immessi (consumi di materie prime)
* Codifica di modelli, aspetti, prodotti finiti in Stealth 400/PLM

**PROFILO**

* Conoscenza base delle distinte basi e dei cicli di lavorazione
* Preferibile conoscenza di Stealth400, Stealth3000, PLM
* Buona conoscenza di Excel e del pacchetto office
* Buone capacità organizzative,
* Precisione ed attenzione al dettaglio
* Teamwork
* Buone capacità di problem solving
* Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese

*In Saint Laurent ci impegniamo a costruire una forza lavoro diversificata. Crediamo che la diversità in tutte le sue forme –generi, età, nazionalità, cultura, credenze religiose e orientamento sessuale \- arricchisca il posto di lavoro. Offre nuove opportunità alle persone per esprimere il loro talento, come individuo o come parte di un gruppo, ed aiuta a promuovere la nostra abilità di adattarci in un mondo che cambia velocemente. È una opportunità aperta a tutti e noi considereremo tutte le applicazioni che verranno inviate da qualsiasi candidato qualificato.*

**Job Type**

Regular
**Start Date**

2026\-04\-15
**Schedule**

Full time
**Organization**

Yves Saint Laurent Manifatture S.r.l

title: Responsabile Amministrativo
description:

Si richiede capacità di gestire la contabilità generale e le attività di bilancio, con conoscenze delle normative fiscali e amministrative e familiarità con l'utilizzo di software contabili e strumenti di gestione aziendale, nonché capacità di lavorare con precisione.

Contratto di lavoro: Tempo pieno

Retribuzione: a partire da €25\.000,00 all'anno

title: ESTETISTO/A ONICOTECNICA/O
description:
Sei appassionata/o del mondo beauty, ami la precisione e cerchi un ambiente di lavoro dinamico e professionale?

**Chi stiamo cercando:**  
Siamo alla ricerca di una figura solare e qualificata da inserire nel nostro staff. Le tue attività principali saranno:

* **Onicotecnica/o:** Manicure, Pedicure (estetico e curativo), Semipermanente, Ricostruzione in Gel e Nail Art.
* **Estetica Base:** Epilazione, Sopracciglia, Trattamenti Viso e corpo
* **Consulenza:** Accoglienza delle clienti e consiglio sui prodotti di mantenimento domiciliare.

**Cosa vorremmo trovare in te:**

* Qualifica professionale di Estetista.
* Esperienza pregressa nel ruolo (anche minima, ma con tanta voglia di imparare).
* Precisione estrema e attenzione all'igiene.
* Puntualità e capacità di lavorare in team.

title: Junior Service Delivery Consultant
company_description: Nós existimos para conectar as pessoas, negócios e a sociedade, servindo e encantando todos os dias.

title: Help Desk Specialist (P.IVA)
description:
**Descrizione del ruolo**  
Cogent Networks è alla ricerca di un IT Support Engineer qualificato per supportare uno dei nostri clienti con sede a Venezia. Il ruolo prevede la fornitura di supporto IT on\-site per tre giorni a settimana su base fissa, per una durata di un anno. È richiesta la conoscenza fluente della lingua italiana per comunicare efficacemente con utenti e stakeholder locali.

**Responsabilità principali**

* Fornire supporto tecnico di secondo livello e servizi di Help Desk, assistendo gli utenti nella risoluzione di problematiche IT.
* Installare, configurare e mantenere desktop, laptop, stampanti e altre periferiche IT, oltre alle applicazioni desktop.
* Effettuare interventi tecnici on\-site presso diverse sedi del cliente.
* Diagnosticare e risolvere problematiche hardware e software su ambienti Windows, macOS e Linux (la conoscenza dei dispositivi Apple/macOS è considerata un plus).
* Supportare e gestire reti LAN/WAN, inclusi TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, VPN e RDP.
* Fornire supporto su periferiche e dispositivi di rete quali monitor, tastiere, stampanti, router, switch e firewall.
* Configurare dispositivi RF e gestire stampanti specializzate (es. stampanti termiche e Zebra).
* Utilizzare strumenti come VDI, SCCM/LANDesk/Altiris, Skype for Business e OneDrive.
* Gestire dispositivi mobili tramite strumenti MDM (es. MobileIron, Intune), inclusa configurazione email, autenticazione RSA e VPN.
* Utilizzare sistemi di ticketing per registrare, prioritizzare e risolvere richieste di supporto.
* Applicare capacità analitiche e di troubleshooting per risolvere problemi tecnici complessi, inclusi problemi di cablaggio lato utente.

**Requisiti**

* Laurea in Informatica, Ingegneria Informatica o discipline affini.
* 1–2 anni di esperienza in ruoli di Help Desk o supporto tecnico.
* Certificazione CompTIA A\+ o altre certificazioni IT rilevanti (preferibile).
* Esperienza con sistemi di ticketing (es. ServiceNow).
* Ottime capacità di problem solving e attitudine al lavoro sia in autonomia che in team.
* Eccellenti capacità comunicative e relazionali, con abilità di interagire con stakeholder tecnici e non tecnici.
* Conoscenza fluente della lingua italiana.
* Disponibilità a lavorare con contratto B2B; è richiesta partita IVA (solo freelance).

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-

**Company Description**  
Cogent Networks is a team of dedicated IT professionals with over 10 years of combined experience in delivering high\-quality IT services. Our skilled specialists support businesses in planning, developing, implementing, and managing technology solutions. We offer a comprehensive, one\-stop service covering Managed IT Services, Software Development, and Digital Marketing.

**Role Description**  
Cogent Networks is looking for a skilled IT Support Engineer to support one of our clients based in Venice. This role involves providing on\-site IT support on a fixed schedule of three days per week for a duration of one year. Fluency in Italian is essential to effectively communicate with local users and stakeholders.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Provide second\-level technical support and Help Desk services, assisting users with IT\-related issues.
* Install, configure, and maintain desktops, laptops, printers, and other IT peripherals, as well as desktop applications.
* Perform on\-site technical interventions across multiple client locations.
* Diagnose and resolve hardware and software issues across Windows, macOS, and Linux environments (experience with macOS/Apple devices is a plus).
* Support and troubleshoot LAN/WAN environments, including TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, VPN, and RDP.
* Provide support for peripherals and network devices such as monitors, keyboards, printers, routers, switches, and firewalls.
* Configure RF devices and manage specialized printers (e.g., thermal and Zebra printers).
* Utilize tools such as VDI, SCCM/LANDesk/Altiris, Skype for Business, and OneDrive.
* Manage mobile devices using MDM tools (e.g., MobileIron, Intune), including email setup, RSA authentication, and VPN configuration.
* Use ticketing systems to log, prioritize, and resolve incidents and service requests.
* Apply strong analytical and troubleshooting skills to resolve complex technical and network issues, including end\-user cabling problems.

**Requirements**

* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
* 1–2 years of experience in a Help Desk or technical support role.
* CompTIA A\+ certification or other relevant IT certifications preferred.
* Experience with ticketing systems (e.g., ServiceNow).
* Strong problem\-solving skills and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage both technical and non\-technical stakeholders.
* Fluency in Italian is required.
* Availability to work under a B2B contract; candidates must have a valid VAT/tax number (freelancers only).

**Why Join Us**  
Join Cogent Networks and become part of a dynamic, client\-focused team committed to delivering high\-quality IT support and services.

Contratto di lavoro: Contratto con partita IVA

Retribuzione: Fino a €150,00 al giorno

Sede di lavoro: Di persona

title: Director Quality
description:
**QUALITY DIRECTOR**

***Would you like to work in an international environment where you’ll make a difference every day? At Permobil, we’re looking for game\-changers to join us as we innovate for individuals and develop the world’s most advanced assistive solutions.***


At **Permobil**, the people who use our products come first. Our purpose is to create advanced assistive solutions that make the lives of adults and children living with disabilities more enriching. Our people are passionate about pushing the boundaries of technology to deliver innovations that improve lives. We are a sustainable and responsible business committed to continuous improvement, and our customers can trust us to provide market\-leading solutions driven by insights\-based clinical data.

**Position Overview**


The **Quality Director** is the senior quality leader for the Progeo (Italy) and Supportec (Netherlands) manufacturing sites, and for product development and design assurance for Progeo. It is accountable for regulatory compliance, product quality and continuous improvement, leading a robust QMS across the full product lifecycle, including design transfer, change implementation, audit readiness and the compliant adoption of automation and AI‑enabled manufacturing technologies.


As part of the site leadership team, the role partners closely with Product Innovation (R\&D) and Operations to ensure efficient, compliant design transfer in an agile environment. The Quality Director also leads audit readiness and regulatory compliance, acting as PRRC and Management Representative in line with ISO 13485, 21 CFR 820, and EU MDR, while driving a strong culture of quality and continuous improvement.

*This is a hybrid position, based in Treviso.*

**Key Responsibilities**

* Lead and ensure an effective, compliant Quality Management System (QMS) aligned with ISO 13485, FDA 21 CFR 820, EU MDR, and global standards; act as Management Representative and EU PRRC.
* Oversee core QMS processes (e.g., CAPA, non\-conformance, audits, change control, complaints, validation, risk management) and drive continuous improvement.
* Own quality oversight of design transfer and post\-market design changes, ensuring compliant and efficient implementation into manufacturing.
* Partner with Product Innovation (R\&D) and Operations to ensure manufacturability, risk mitigation, validation, and full traceability of design changes.
* Provide quality leadership to manufacturing, including investigations, CAPA, product acceptance, and maintaining validated, controlled processes.
* Ensure compliant implementation and validation of automation, digital systems, and AI\-enabled technologies, with strong data integrity practices.
* Lead audit and inspection readiness; act as primary quality contact for regulatory inspections and external audits, ensuring timely responses.
* Drive continuous improvement and quality culture through Lean, Six Sigma, and data\-driven decision\-making.
* Build strong cross\-functional partnerships and co\-own manufacturing quality performance, including cost of poor quality (COPQ).
* Lead, develop, and coach the Quality team; establish and monitor key quality metrics and report performance to leadership.

**Qualifications \& Skills:**

* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering, Life Sciences or a related discipline is required; a Master’s degree is preferred.
* At least 10 years of experience in quality and/or regulatory roles in the medical device industry, including significant experience in manufacturing environments.
* Proven track record as a leader of quality at site or regional level, including direct leadership of a QA/RA team and ownership of QMS processes for a legal manufacturer.
* Demonstrated experience serving as QA/RA leader for a medical device manufacturer, including authority to approve technical documents and respond to regulators and certifying agencies
* Significant experience leading regulatory inspections and third party audits at production sites, including ISO 13485 and managing responses to findings.
* Fulfilling PRRC requirements according to MDR Article 15 and is eligible to be formally designated as PRRC for the legal manufacturer.
* Deep working knowledge of ISO 13485, FDA 21 CFR 820, EU MDR 2017/745 and related medical device regulatory requirements.
* Quality \& regulatory leadership in medical device development and manufacturing (preferred: multi site operations).
* Strong knowledge of quality systems and quality assurance within a regulated industry (preferably medical devices).
* Experience supporting frequent design transfers and/or post market design changes in a high change manufacturing environment.
* Experience with automation, AI or digital manufacturing technologies and associated validation and data integrity expectations.
* Regulatory judgment in fast moving environments; risk based, systems level thinking; strong operations partnership and influence skills; data driven and digitally fluent quality leadership.
* Fluency in English (written and spoken) is required; additional languages are an advantage.

**Why is Permobil the next step for you?**

* **You’ll make a difference. Every day.**
  

Everything we do leads to understanding and improving the lives of our users. Through our evidence\-based innovation, we make a difference to people’s lives.
* **You’ll make your mark as part of our future.**
  

We collaborate with colleagues across borders to Innovate for Individuals. The impact you make personally could lead change around the world.
* **You’ll feel welcome from day one.**
  

We’re known for being great colleagues, who are collaborative, fun and at the cutting\-edge. Everyone at Permobil cares as much as you do about making a positive difference.

**Application Process**


We will conduct selection continuously so send us your application today or before **April 30th** at the latest!  

For information about the recruitment process, please contact Zivile Mitkute, Talent Acquisition Partner, zivile.mitkute@permobil.com


Permobil is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals.


Please respect that we do not wish recruitment assistance or advertising, we decline calls from recruitment and advertising providers.

**More about Permobil**


Permobil founder Dr. Per Uddén believed that helping people achieve the greatest level of independence is a basic human right and, for over 50 years, Permobil has held fast to that belief. Permobil is a global leader in advanced rehabilitation technology, passionate about better understanding our users’ needs and improving their quality of life through state\-of\-the\-art healthcare solutions. Today, those solutions include power wheelchairs, seating and positioning products, power assist, and manual wheelchairs.


Permobil is part of Patricia Industries, a subsidiary of Investor AB, and is headquartered in Sweden. Permobil has 2 000 team members in 18 countries around the world. For more information regarding the company’s storied history and complete product line, visit permobil.com.

title: Veneto, Friuli Venezia Giulia e Trentino Alto Adige Certification Industrial Services Inspector
description:
**Posting Date:** 31 Mar 2026
**City:** Venezia
**Location:**Venezia, IT, 30175 San Bonifacio, IT, 37047
**Contract Type:** Permanent
**Division:** Mediterranean \& Africa Certification
**Level of experience:** Intermediate
RINA is currently recruiting for a **Veneto, Friuli Venezia Giulia e Trentino Alto Adige Certification Industrial Services Inspector** to join its office in San Bonifacio or Venezia, Italywithin the **Mediterranean \& Africa Certification** Division.
**Mission**

The role is focuses on ensuring the safety and quality of industrial services (e.g. welding, pressure equipment, personnel certification and CE marking) helping businesses meet regulatory standards and deliver reliable, safe services to their customers. The role works under the supervision on more complex assignments. \#LI\-MP3**Key Accountabilities**
* Conduct Inspections: Perform on\-site inspections, examinations and audits to assess compliance with applicable codes, standards, and regulations.
* Documentation and Reporting: Prepare detailed inspection, examination and audit reports, documenting findings and providing clear recommendations for corrective actions or improvements.
* Maintain accurate records of inspections, approvals, and rejections.
* Material and Procedure Review: Review materials, manufacturing/process procedures and testing methods (e.g. NDT) to ensure they meet certification requirements.
* Quality Assurance: Monitor the quality control processes during the activities, ensuring adherence to quality control plans.
* Code Compliance: Stay up to date with industry codes, standards, and regulations to ensure that all inspections or examinations are conducted in accordance with the latest requirements.
* Qualification: Assess the qualifications and certifications of services and operators, ensuring they meet the appropriate standards.
* Safety: Promote and enforce safety measures, ensuring that the operations are conducted in a safe and compliant manner. Evaluate the reliability of equipment and plant inspected with tests and measures to ensure a compliance safety.
* Client Interaction: Communicate with clients, answer their questions competently, and providing information about the certification scheme.
* Continuous Improvement: Identify opportunities for process improvements and contribute to enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of inspection procedures.
* Training: Stay current with the latest inspection techniques and technologies and provide training to colleagues and clients as needed.
**Qualifications**
* Proven experience in industrial service inspection, particularly in areas such as welding, pressure equipment, personnel certification, and CE marking.
* Knowledge of regulatory standards and mandatory certifications relevant to industrial services (e.g. pressure vessels, piping, valves, etc.).
* Strong understanding of quality assurance, safety regulations, and technical standards applicable to industrial services and equipment (ASME Codes, EN standards, etc)
* Ability to read and interpret technical drawings, manuals, and compliance documentation.
* Attention to detail and the ability to assess compliance with safety regulations, codes, and standards.
* Holding ASME Authorized Inspector qualification.
* Proficiency in using inspection tools and test equipment.
* Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, for reporting findings and interacting with clients and team members.
* Ability to work independently and under the supervision of senior inspectors for more complex assignments.
* Strong problem\-solving abilities and the capacity to provide clear recommendations for compliance improvements.
* Certifications or professional membership in relevant bodies (e.g., Welding Engineer, NDT qualifications, CE marking expert) is a plus.
**Education**

Bachelor’s degree in engineering general or valid ASME Inspector qualification  



RINA is a multinational company providing a wide range of services in the energy, marine, certification, infrastructure \& mobility, industry, research \& development sectors. Our business model covers the full process of project development, from concept to completion.  


*At RINA, we endeavor to create a work environment where every single person is valued and encouraged to develop new ideas. We provide equal employment opportunities and are committed to creating a workplace where everyone feels respected and safe from discrimination or harassment of any kind. We are also compliant to the Italian Law n. 68/99\.*
company_description: Join us and build your own path of excellence.

title: Assistant Director of Finance - JW Marriott Venice Resort & Spa
description:
**Additional Information**  

**Job Number**26039447  

**Job Category**Finance \& Accounting  

**Location**JW Marriott Venice Resort \& Spa, Isola delle Rose, Venice, Italy, Italy, 30133  

**Schedule**Full Time  

**Located Remotely?**N  

**Position Type** Management  

**JOB SUMMARY**

  



The Assistant Director of Finance, manages the day\-to\-day operation of the Accounting Office. Primary responsibilities include financial analysis and financial reporting, budgeting/forecasting, audit and control, asset and liability reconciliation, working capital and cash control.

  



**CANDIDATE PROFILE**


Education
* Bachelor’s degree (4\-year) or Master’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related field.
* Additional qualifications such as a master’s in hospitality finance or professional certifications (e.g., CFA, CPA) are considered a plus.

Experience
* Strong finance and accounting experience, high preference within the hotel or luxury hospitality sector.
* Hands\-on experience with budgeting, forecasting, financial reporting, and financial control.
* Familiarity with hotel management systems (e.g., Opera, Fidelio, Micros) or similar ERP systems.
* Experience supervising accounting teams or collaborating closely with Sales, Revenue, and Operations departments.

Key Skills
* Advanced financial analysis and strong attention to detail
* Understanding of hotel KPIs such as RevPAR, GOP, NOI
* Excellent communication and cross\-departmental collaboration skills
* Knowledge of local and international accounting and tax regulations
  

  

**CORE WORK ACTIVITIES**


Assisting in Management of Accounting Team


* Utilizes interpersonal and communication skills to lead, influence, and encourage others; advocates sound financial/business decision making; demonstrates honesty/integrity; leads by example.


* Oversees internal, external and regulatory audit processes.


* Ensures that regular on\-going communication occurs with employees to create awareness of business objectives, communicate expectations, and recognize performance.


* Celebrates successes by publicly recognizing the contributions of team members.


* Establishes and maintains open, collaborative relationships with employees.


* Provides excellent leadership (e.g., differentiates top performers, fosters teamwork and encourages work/life balance).


* Ensures employees establish and maintain open, collaborative relationships within their team.


* Participates in the employee performance appraisal process, providing feedback as needed.


* Ensures disciplinary procedures and documentation are completed according to Standard and Local Operating Procedures (SOPs and LSOPs) and support the Peer Review Process.


* Ensures property policies are administered fairly and consistently.


* Utilizes an "open door" policy.


* Solicits employee feedback.

  



**Managing Projects and Policies**


* Generates and provides accurate and timely results in the form of reports, presentations, etc.


* Analyzes information and evaluates results to choose the best solution and solve problems.


* Compiles, codes, categorizes, calculates, tabulates, audits, or verifies information or data.


* Ensures that the P\&L is accurate (i.e., costs are properly matched to revenue, costs are recorded in the proper accounts) and statements are delivered to appropriate individuals in a timely manner.


* Reconciles balance sheet.


* Ensures tax rates used for sales/use tax are current and proper amounts are collected and/or accrued.


* Ensures compliance with standard and local operating procedures (SOPs and LSOPs).


* Ensures account balances are supported by appropriate documentation in accordance with SOPs.


* Reviews audit issues and makes corrections as necessary.


* Ensures property permits, licenses and if applicable vendor contracts are current.


* Leverages centralized accounting processes and shared services.

  



**Demonstrating and Applying Accounting Knowledge**


* Demonstrates knowledge of job\-relevant issues, products, systems, and processes.


* Keeps up\-to\-date technically and applying new knowledge to your job.


* Uses computers and computer systems (including hardware and software) to program, write software, set up functions, enter data, or process information.


* Uses relevant information and individual judgment to determine whether events or processes comply with laws, regulations, or standards.


* Leverages technology and effectively uses information systems and tools to generate financial reports and provide managers with analytical support to drive decision\-making.

  



**Proving Financial Information and Guidance to Others**


* Informs and/or updates the executives, the peers and the subordinates on relevant financial information in a timely manner.


* Attends critique meetings to review information with management team.


* Advises the Director of Finance on existing and evolving operating/financial issues.


* Communicates financial concepts in a clear and persuasive manner that is easy to understand and drives desired behaviors.


* Provide direction and assistance to other organizational units regarding accounting and budgeting policies and procedures, and efficient control and utilization of financial resources.


* Provides information to supervisors, co\-workers, and subordinates by telephone, in written form, e\-mail, or in person.

  



**Maintaining Finance and Accounting Goals**


* Submits reports in a timely manner, ensuring delivery deadlines.


* Ensures profits and losses are documented accurately.


* Achieves and exceeds goals including performance goals, budget goals, team goals, etc.


* Develops specific goals and plans to prioritize, organize, and accomplish your work.


* Monitors all taxes that apply, ensuring that taxes are current, collected and/or accrued.


* Supports a strong accounting \& operational control environment to safeguard assets, improve operations and profitability and manage business risks.
  

*At Marriott International, we are dedicated to being an equal opportunity employer, welcoming all and providing access to opportunity. We actively foster an environment where the unique backgrounds of our associates are valued and celebrated. Our greatest strength lies in the rich blend of culture, talent, and experiences of our associates. We are committed to non\-discrimination on any protected basis, including disability, veteran status, or other basis protected by applicable law.*


Marriott Hotels strive to elevate the art of hospitality, innovating at every opportunity while keeping the comfort of the oh\-so\-familiar all around the globe. As a host with Marriott Hotels, you will help keep the promise of “Wonderful Hospitality. Always.” by delivering thoughtful, heartfelt, forward\-thinking service that upholds and builds upon this living legacy. With the name that’s synonymous with hospitality the world over, we are proud to welcome you to explore a career with Marriott Hotels. In joining Marriott Hotels, you join a portfolio of brands with Marriott International. **Be** where you can do your best work, **begin** your purpose, **belong** to an amazing global team, and **become** the best version of you.  




JW Marriott is part of Marriott International's luxury portfolio and consists of more than 100 beautiful properties in gateway cities and distinctive resort locations around the world. JW believes our associates come first. Because if you’re happy, our guests will be happy. JW Marriott associates are confident, innovative, genuine, intuitive, and carry on the legacy of the brand’s namesake and company founder, J.Willard Marriott. Our hotels offer a work experience unlike any other, where you’ll be part of a community and enjoy true camaraderie with a diverse group of co\-workers. JW creates opportunities for training, development, recognition and most importantly, a place where you can pursue your passions in a luxury environment with a focus on holistic well\-being. Treating guests exceptionally starts with the way we take care of our associates. That’s The JW Treatment™. In joining JW Marriott, you join a portfolio of brands with Marriott International. **Be** where you can do your best work, **begin** your purpose, **belong** to an amazing global team, and **become** the best version of you.

title: Customer support representative
description:
**Position Overview**
---------------------


The Customer Support Representative is responsible for providing exceptional service to customers by handling inquiries, resolving issues, and ensuring customer satisfaction. This role requires strong communication skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work collaboratively with internal teams to deliver timely and accurate solutions.

**Job Duties and Responsibilities**
-----------------------------------

* Respond promptly and professionally to customer inquiries via email, phone, and other communication channels.
* Provide accurate information regarding products, services, and order status.
* Manage and resolve customer complaints and issues in a timely manner.
* Coordinate with internal departments (Sales, Logistics, Quality, Engineering) to ensure customer needs are met.
* Process orders, returns, and replacements according to company policies.
* Handle invoicing activities and ensure accurate billing for customer orders.
* Manage shipping processes for intra\-EU and extra\-EU deliveries, ensuring compliance with customs and regulatory requirements.
* Monitor and follow up on open cases to ensure resolution and customer satisfaction.
* Identify opportunities to improve processes and enhance the customer experience.
**Education and Qualifications**
--------------------------------

* High school diploma or equivalent; a degree in Business Administration or related field is a plus.
* Proven experience in customer service or a similar role.
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English.
* Strong problem\-solving and organizational skills.
* Proficiency in MS Office suite.
* Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment and manage multiple priorities
**Skills and Competencies**
---------------------------

* Customer\-oriented mindset with a focus on delivering high\-quality service.
* Team player with strong interpersonal skills.
* Attention to detail and accuracy.
* Adaptability and resilience under pressure
company_description: At Dana, we believe the talent of our people power a customer-centric organization that is continuously improving the performance and the efficiency of vehicles and machines around the globe.

title: EMEA Controls Deployment Leader – Thermal Management
description:
**Who are we?**

At Vertiv, we’re on a mission to empower the people that will power the future. From a simple swipe to life\-changing medicines, from push notifications to generative AI. We design, manufacture, and service the products and solutions that keep the world connected. With $6\.9 billion in sales, a strong customer base and global reach spanning nearly 70 countries, we are uniquely positioned to deliver greater value to our customers and create new opportunities for our people.  



We are currently seeking a **Controls Deployment Leader** to join our dynamic team in **Piove di Sacco (PD), Italy!**

The Controls Deployment Leader – Thermal Management (EMEA) is responsible for the execution and delivery of control systems supporting Vertiv’s thermal management solutions across data center projects in the EMEA region. This role drives standardization, quality, and performance of controls for air\- and liquid\-based cooling technologies, ensuring reliable operation in mission\-critical environments. The position partners closely with engineering, commissioning, and field teams to deliver scalable, high\-efficiency thermal solutions.  



**What kind of work will you be doing?**

* Lead deployment of control systems across Vertiv Thermal Management solutions, including:
	+ Chilled water and DX thermal systems
	+ CRAH/CRAC units
	+ Air handling and economization systems
	+ Liquid cooling technologies
* Execute standardized control architectures, sequences of operation, and alarm strategies aligned with Vertiv global engineering standards
* Support cross\-functional teams including Thermal Engineering, Integrated Solutions, Project Management, Commissioning, and Field Service
* Oversee control panel configuration, PLC programming, network integration, and system validation
* Lead FAT, SAT, and Level 3–5 commissioning activities, ensuring systems meet design intent and performance requirements
* Support for integration with customer BMS/DCIM platforms when requested.
* Drive optimization of thermal performance, redundancy strategies (N\+1, 2N), and energy efficiency (PUE targets)
* **Provide on\-site technical leadership during commissioning, startup, and customer handover**
* Identify and resolve field issues, minimizing rework and ensuring first\-pass success
Capture lessons learned and drive continuous improvement across EMEA deployments  
* 

**What will make you successful?**

* Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical, Electrical, or Controls Engineering (or equivalent experience)
* 7\+ years of experience in HVAC controls, data center cooling systems, or mission\-critical infrastructure
* Hands\-on experience with PLCs, industrial control systems, and communication protocols (BACnet, Modbus, TCP/IP)
* Proven experience deploying thermal systems within data centers or critical environments
* Knowledge of chilled water systems, DX cooling, and airflow management
* Experience with various thermal deployments
* Familiarity with hyperscale and colocation customers
* Experience working across multiple EMEA countries and contractor environments
* Strong execution and ownership mindset aligned with Vertiv delivery culture
* Deep technical expertise in thermal management systems and controls
* Ability to lead cross\-functional and geographically distributed teams
* Effective communication across customers, partners, and internal stakeholders
* Commitment to safety, quality, and operational excellence

**Key Metrics**

* On\-time delivery of Thermal Management controls deployments across EMEA
* First\-pass commissioning success rate (L1–L5\)
* Thermal system performance and uptime compliance
* Reduction in field defects and rework
* Customer satisfaction and successful project handover

**Work Environment**

* Field\-intensive role with up to 50% travel across EMEA data center sites
* Work in live, mission\-critical environments with strict operational and safety requirements
* Flexibility to support multi\-country deployments and varying project schedules

**Additional Considerations (EMEA)**

* Fluency in English required; additional European or regional languages preferred
* Familiarity with EU directives (CE marking, Low Voltage Directive) and regional compliance frameworks
Experience working with diverse contractor ecosystems and permitting processes across EMEA  
* 

If **YOU** are the person we are looking for, feel free to apply and let's start drafting your future career in a healthy and growing environment!  



The successful candidate will embrace Vertiv’s Core Principals \& Behaviors to help execute our Strategic Priorities.  



**OUR CORE PRINCIPALS** : Safety. Integrity. Respect. Teamwork. Diversity \& Inclusion.  



**OUR STRATEGIC PRIORITIES**

* Customer Focus
* Operational Excellence
* High\-Performance Culture
* Innovation
Financial Strength  
* 

**OUR BEHAVIORS**

* Own It
* Act With Urgency
* Foster a Customer\-First Mindset
* Think Big and Execute
* Lead by Example
* Drive Continuous Improvement
Learn and Seek Out Development  
* 

\#LI\-DP1  



Vertiv is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. We promote equal opportunities for all with respect to hiring, terms of employment, mobility, training, compensation, and occupational health, without discrimination as to age, race, color, religion, creed, sex, pregnancy status (including childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical conditions), marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression (including transgender status or sexual stereotypes), genetic information, citizenship status, national origin, protected veteran status, political affiliation, or disability. If you have a disability and are having difficulty accessing or using this website to apply for a position, you can request help by sending an email to help.join@vertivco.com . If you are interested in applying or learning more about this role, please visit the company’s career page located on Vertiv.com/Careers
